translate.en: The soup is cold.
She is an architect.
Are you okay?
The meeting is at noon.
translate.es: La reunión es a las doce.

translate.en: The store is closed on Sundays.
translate.es: La tienda está cerrada los domingos.

translate.en: He is nervous about the exam.
They are from Argentina.
The window is broken.
It is very important to study.
We are very tired after the trip.
translate.es: Estamos muy cansados después del viaje.

pairs.en: I used to eat / was eating
it was raining when I left
he lived in Paris for 3 years
he used to live near the park
I had a lot of money (state)
I tried to go but couldn't
he used to scream as a child

fillblank.question: Cuando era niño, ___ (jugar) en el parque todos los días.
El tren ___ (llegar) con dos horas de retraso.
Ella ___ (conocer) a su marido en la universidad.
De pequeño siempre ___ (tener) miedo de la oscuridad.
Nosotros ___ (vivir) en México durante diez años.

translate.en: I was reading when the phone rang.
translate.es: Estaba leyendo cuando sonó el teléfono.

translate.en: Every summer we used to go to the beach.
translate.es: Todos los veranos íbamos a la playa.

translate.en: She finished the project last night.
translate.explanation: Completed event with time marker = preterite

translate.en: When I was young I played football.
translate.es: Cuando era joven jugaba al fútbol.

translate.en: He didn't say anything.
It was raining and the streets were empty.
translate.es: Llovía y las calles estaban vacías.

translate.en: I met him for the first time in 2010.
translate.es: Lo conocí por primera vez en 2010.

translate.en: She used to work in a bank.
We ate at that restaurant three times.
translate.es: Comimos en ese restaurante tres veces.

translate.en: He was tired because he hadn't slept.
translate.es: Estaba cansado porque no había dormido.

pairs.es: busco un apartamento que sea barato
pairs.en: I'm looking for an apartment that is cheap
I need you to help me

pairs.es: no hay nadie que hable chino
pairs.en: there's no one who speaks Chinese
I ask you to be honest

fillblank.question: Quiero que tú me ___ (decir) la verdad.
fillblank.hint: I want you to tell me

translate.en: I want you to be happy.
It's strange that he isn't here.
translate.es: Es extraño que no esté aquí.

translate.en: Maybe they know the answer.
I'll call you when I arrive.
She needs someone who can help her.
translate.es: Necesita a alguien que pueda ayudarla.

translate.en: I hope you feel better soon.
It's important that you rest.
I doubt that it will rain today.
She told me to arrive early.
Before you leave, turn off the lights.
translate.es: Antes de que te vayas, apaga las luces.

pairs.en: because of you / for your sake
pairs.explanation: Por = on behalf of / because of

pairs.en: to work in order to live

pairs.explanation: Por = on behalf of a cause

fillblank.question: Ella habla muy bien ___ ser extranjera.
Lo hago ___ ti, no ___ él.
fillblank.explanation: Acting on behalf of someone = por

translate.en: I bought this for you.
He did it because of love.
The homework is due Friday.
translate.es: Los deberes son para el viernes.

translate.en: They walked through the park.
She studies hard in order to pass.
I exchanged my car for a bike.
translate.es: Cambié mi coche por una bici.

translate.en: This medicine is for headaches.
translate.es: Esta medicina es para el dolor de cabeza.

translate.en: Thanks for everything.
For a student he works a lot.
I'll be there for two weeks.

pairs.en: tomorrow I'm going to the cinema

translate.en: I go to the gym every Monday.
translate.es: Voy al gimnasio todos los lunes.

translate.en: She knows three languages.
What do you want to eat?
We leave at eight every morning.
translate.es: Salimos a las ocho todas las mañanas.

translate.en: Can you help me?
There is a problem.
I'm meeting a friend tomorrow.
He always says the right thing.
Do you have time?
I make dinner every night.
translate.es: Hago la cena todas las noches.

pairs.en: I feel like an ice cream

fillblank.question: A nosotros ___ las películas de acción.
A mí me ___ (doler) las piernas después de correr.
A los estudiantes ___ los exámenes sorpresa.
fillblank.explanation: Exámenes = plural → gustan; students = les (plural indirect object).

fillblank.question: ¿A vosotros os ___ (parecer) buena idea?
fillblank.hint: does it seem like a good idea to you all (singular idea)
fillblank.explanation: Parecer follows the gustar pattern; 'una buena idea' is singular → parece.

translate.en: I like Spanish food a lot.
translate.es: Me gusta mucho la comida española.

translate.en: Do you like traveling?
He loves his dog.
They don't like cold weather.
My feet hurt.
She's interested in art.
We really like these songs.
I feel like a coffee.
My parents don't like loud music.
translate.es: A mis padres no les gusta la música alta.
translate.explanation: La música (singular) is the subject → gusta; parents = les.

translate.en: It seems like a great plan to me.
translate.explanation: Parecer follows gustar's structure: singular subject (un plan) → parece, me = indirect object.

pairs.explanation: Exception: mano ends in -o but is feminine
Feminine noun uses el before stressed a-

fillblank.question: Necesito ___ (el/la/un/una) agua fría.
fillblank.explanation: Agua is feminine but takes el/un before it

fillblank.question: ___ (el/la) clima de esta región es muy seco.
fillblank.hint: clima ends in -a but is masculine
fillblank.explanation: Clima is one of the Greek-origin -ma words that are masculine: el clima.

fillblank.question: Sus manos estaban heladas y ___.
fillblank.explanation: Manos is feminine plural → the adjective must be feminine plural: rojas.

translate.en: The red houses are beautiful.
I need a new white shirt.
The blue cars are fast.
She is a good doctor.
The big problems are difficult.
Give me cold water please.
A new red dress.
The interesting books.
translate.explanation: Interesante = no gender change, only number

translate.en: The climate here is very dry.
translate.es: El clima aquí es muy seco.
translate.explanation: Clima = masculine despite -a ending → el clima.

translate.en: She has cold, red hands.
translate.es: Tiene las manos frías y rojas.
translate.explanation: Manos = feminine → frías, rojas (feminine plural adjectives).

fillblank.question: Yo ___ (levantarse) a las seis de la mañana.
¿A qué hora ___ (acostarse) tú?
fillblank.hint: what time do you go to bed

fillblank.question: Yo ___ (ponerse) el abrigo porque hace frío.
¿Cómo ___ (llevarse) tú con tus hermanos?
fillblank.hint: how do you get along with your siblings
fillblank.explanation: Llevarse bien/mal = to get along; tú → te llevas.

fillblank.question: Nosotros ___ (quejarse) del ruido todas las noches.

translate.en: She washes her hair every morning.
translate.es: Se lava el pelo todas las mañanas.

translate.en: I'm getting angry.
They met each other at university.
He got up late and missed the bus.
translate.es: Se levantó tarde y perdió el autobús.

translate.en: Put your shoes on!
We fell asleep during the film.
Don't worry about it.
I feel much better today.
He and his brother don't get along.
translate.es: Él y su hermano no se llevan bien.

translate.en: I'm going to bed early tonight.
translate.es: Me voy a acostar temprano esta noche. / Voy a acostarme temprano esta noche.
translate.explanation: Reflexive pronoun goes before the conjugated verb OR attaches to the infinitive.

pairs.en: Speak more slowly, please. (to a friend)
Speak more slowly, please. (to a stranger)
Can you help me with this? (informal)
Can you help me with this? (formal)

fillblank.question: Perdón, ¿___ (llamarse, usted) María?
fillblank.explanation: Usted conjugates like él/ella: se llama.

fillblank.question: Oye, ¿___ (llamarse, tú) Ana?
fillblank.hint: is your name Ana? (informal, to a friend)

fillblank.question: Señora, ¿es este ___ (posesivo) paraguas?
fillblank.hint: is this your umbrella? (formal, to a woman)
fillblank.explanation: Usted's possessive is su/sus, same as his/her/their.

fillblank.question: Profesor, ¿___ (poder, usted) ayudarme?
fillblank.hint: can you help me? (formal, to a professor)

fillblank.question: Señor, ___ (sentarse, comando usted) aquí, por favor.
fillblank.hint: have a seat here, please (formal command)

translate.en: What's your name? (asking a classmate)
What's your name? (asking your doctor)
Is this your car? (asking your neighbor's elderly mother)
translate.explanation: Usted possessive su — showing respect toward an elder.

translate.en: Is this your car? (asking your little cousin)
translate.explanation: Tú possessive tu — informal family context.

translate.en: Please come in, sir.
translate.explanation: Usted command of pasar → pase — formal register matching 'señor'.

translate.en: Come in, come in! (to your best friend)
translate.explanation: Tú command of pasar → pasa — informal register.

translate.en: Could you repeat that, please? (to a stranger on the phone)
translate.explanation: Podría = formal/usted conditional — extra-polite request to a stranger.

translate.en: Can you repeat that? (to your brother)
translate.explanation: Puedes = tú present — casual request among family.

pairs.explanation: Negation before object pronoun before verb
Adjectives usually follow noun in Spanish
Some adjectives change meaning before noun
Pronoun before aux OR attached to gerund
Solo modifies the verb, placed before it

fillblank.question: ___ veo a María todos los días. (I see)
fillblank.explanation: Direct object pronoun precedes conjugated verb

fillblank.question: No ___ gusta. (me)
fillblank.hint: me goes between no and verb

fillblank.question: Ella tiene los ojos ___. (blue, not azules ojos)
— ¿Quién ganó el partido? — ___ nuestro equipo.
fillblank.hint: new information (the subject) comes after the verb
fillblank.explanation: As an answer, the new information (the subject) is placed after the verb: ganó nuestro equipo.

fillblank.question: Es un ___ amigo mío, nos conocemos desde niños. (old friend, not old in age)
fillblank.explanation: Viejo amigo (before the noun) = longtime friend; amigo viejo (after) would mean an elderly friend.

fillblank.question: A Pedro ___ vi en el supermercado ayer.
fillblank.hint: fronted direct object (a Pedro) needs a pronoun placeholder
fillblank.explanation: When the direct object is fronted for emphasis, Spanish still requires the clitic pronoun (lo) before the verb.

fillblank.question: Nunca ___ digo mentiras a mis amigos.
fillblank.hint: negative adverb + indirect object pronoun + verb
fillblank.explanation: Negative words like nunca come first, then the object pronoun, then the conjugated verb.

fillblank.question: ¿A qué hora ___ el vuelo?
fillblank.explanation: In questions, the verb typically comes right after the question phrase, with the subject (el vuelo) placed after or omitted.

translate.en: I don't know him.
Where are you from?
She's giving it to me.
translate.es: Me lo está dando. / Está dándomelo.

translate.en: I want to eat it.
I'm looking for a big apartment.
He never calls me.
What time does the train leave?
It was Ana who called, not Carlos.
translate.es: Fue Ana quien llamó, no Carlos.
translate.explanation: Cleft structure (fue...quien) fronts Ana for emphasis as the one who called.

translate.en: I already gave it to them.
translate.explanation: Indirect (les→se) + direct (lo) object pronouns combine and precede the conjugated verb.

translate.en: He's an old friend, we've known each other for years.
translate.es: Es un viejo amigo, nos conocemos desde hace años.
translate.explanation: Pre-noun adjective position (viejo amigo) signals longtime friend, not elderly.

fillblank.question: ¿___ ves a María? — Sí, ___ veo todos los días.
¿Me das el libro? — Sí, te ___ doy.
fillblank.explanation: El libro → lo, te + lo = te lo

fillblank.question: ¿Le diste la carta a Juan? — Sí, ___ ___ di.
fillblank.explanation: Le → se when followed by la

fillblank.question: No ___ entiendo. (you, formal)
fillblank.explanation: Formal you as direct object = lo/la

fillblank.question: ¿Tienes mi número? — Sí, ya ___ tengo guardado.
¿Vas a mandarles el paquete? — Sí, voy a mandár___.
fillblank.hint: les + lo attached to infinitive → se + lo
fillblank.explanation: Le/les + lo becomes se + lo, attached to the end of the infinitive: mandárselo.

fillblank.question: A nosotros no ___ avisaron del cambio.

translate.en: I'll call you tomorrow.
Give it to me.
She told him the truth.
Don't tell them.
I'm going to buy it for her.
translate.explanation: Pronouns attached to infinitive: se + lo

translate.en: He saw us at the park.
Can you show me the way?
translate.es: ¿Puedes mostrarme el camino? / ¿Me puedes mostrar el camino?
translate.explanation: Pronoun before aux or attached to inf

translate.en: I already told her everything.
translate.explanation: Le (to her) + lo (it/everything) → se lo, before the conjugated verb.

translate.en: We're bringing it to you tomorrow.
translate.explanation: Te (to you) + lo (it) both precede the conjugated verb.

translate.en: I haven't seen them since last year.
translate.es: No los he visto desde el año pasado.
translate.explanation: Los = masculine plural direct object, placed before the conjugated auxiliary he.
